text: The surgeon performs a laparoscopic total gastrectomy with DII lymphadenectomy in an obese patient, using four trocars and a liver retractor. The procedure begins with the division of the gastric omentum to mobilize the greater curvature of the stomach, providing access to the lesser sac. Lymphadenectomy of stations IV-D and IV-SB is conducted during this dissection step.

The surgeon begins a clockwise dissection, performing lymphadenectomy of station six using an ultrasonic device. The area is abundant with fat tissue, complicating the identification of gastric block vessels. The gastric epiploid vein is dissected, clipped, and divided, followed by dissection of the gastric epipelagic artery. The surgical field is near the pancreas head.

The surgeon dissects underneath the duodenum to expose the gastroduodenal artery, placing a gauze beneath the duodenum. The dissection proceeds to the hepatic hilum, performing lymphadenectomy at station XII-A. The right gastric artery is isolated and divided, followed by lymphadenectomy at stations V and VIII-A. The left gastric artery is then dissected.

The surgeon clips and divides the short gastric vessels, performing a level IV-SA lymphadenectomy. Station one lymphadenectomy is completed, followed by the removal of station two. The duodenum is stapled using a blue cartridge, and the esophagus is stapled with an articulated vise.

The surgeon performs a Roux-en-Y reconstruction using the Orville device for the esophagojejunostomy, employing a 21 circular stapler for the anastomosis. The staple line is reinforced with 3-0 Prolene sutures, and the jejunal limb is fixed to the mesenteric cross.
The surgeon performs entero-anastomosis using a stapler, followed by closure of mesenteric defects. The adenostomy is completed on the extracted specimen after extending the optical port.
